questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For an isothermal expansion of a perfect gas, the value of $\frac{\Delta P}{P}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Differentiate $P V=$ constant w.r.t $V$
$\Rightarrow P \Delta V+V \Delta P=0 \quad \Rightarrow \frac{\Delta P}{P}=-\frac{\Delta V}{V}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A thermodynamical system is changed from state $\left(\mathrm{P}_{1}, \mathrm{~V}_{1}\right)$ to $\left(\mathrm{P}_{2}, \mathrm{~V}_{2}\right)$ by two different process, the quantity which will remain same will be
questions.solution:
For all process $\Delta \mathrm{U}=\Delta \mathrm{Q}-\Delta \mathrm{W}$
does not change as it depends on initial final states.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: During an adiabatic compression, $830 \mathrm{~J}$ of work is done on 2 moles of a diatomic ideal gas to reduce its volume by $50 \%$. The change in its temperature is nearly: $\left(\mathrm{R}=8.3 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$
questions.solution:
Given : work done, $W=830 \mathrm{~J}$
No. of moles of gas, $\mu=2$
For diatomic gas $\gamma=1.4$
Work done during an adiabatic change
$$
\begin{aligned}
&W=\frac{\mu R\left(T_1-T_2\right)}{\gamma-1} \\
&\Rightarrow 830=\frac{2 \times 8.3(\Delta T)}{1.4-1}=\frac{2 \times 8.3(\Delta T)}{0.4} \\
&\Rightarrow \Delta T=\frac{830 \times 0.4}{2 \times 8.3}=20 \mathrm{~K}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following relations does not give the equation of an adiabatic process, where terms have their usual meaning?
questions.solution:
For adiabatic process,
$p V^{\gamma}=$ constant
For an ideal gas, $p V=n R T$ or $p=\frac{n R T}{V}$
Again, from ideal gas equation,
$$
\begin{aligned}
V &=\frac{n R T}{p} \\
\Rightarrow \quad p\left(\frac{n R T}{p}\right)^{\gamma} &=\text { constant } \\
\Rightarrow \quad p^{1-\gamma} T^{\gamma} &=\text { constant }
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, relation shown in option (d) is not for adiabatic process.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A graph of pressure versus volume for an ideal gas for different processes is as shown. In the graph curve $O C$ represents
questions.solution: (i) Curve OA represents isobaric process (since pressure is constant) Since, the slope of adiabatic process is more steeper than the isothermal process. (ii) Curve OB represents isothermal process. (iii) Curve OC represents adiabatic process. (iv) Curve OD represents isochoric process (since volume is constant).
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
A thermodynamic cycle xyzx is shown on a V - T diagram.

The P - V diagram that best describes this cycle is: (Diagrams are schematic and not to scale)
questions.solution:
According to the ideal equation,

PV=nRT

In the given V‐T diagram,

In process x→y, V∝T. So, the process is isobaric process.

In process y→z, V= constant. So, the process is an isochoric process.

In process z→x, T= constant. So, the process is an isothermal process.

Analyzing using the ideal gas equation in each process, the P‐V graph can be drawn as shown below,
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The mass of oxygen gas occupying a volume of 11.2 litres at a temperature $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and a pressure of $760 \mathrm{~nm}$ of mercury in kilograms is $[$ Molecular weight of oxygen $=32]$
questions.solution:
At STP $(273 \mathrm{~K}$ and at $760 \mathrm{~mm}$ of $\mathrm{Hg})$ the mass of oxygen $=32 \mathrm{~g}$
From
$$
\frac{P_1 V_1}{m_1 T_1}=\frac{P_2 V_2}{m_1 T_2}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& P_1=760 \mathrm{~mm}, V_1=2241 \text { (at STP) } \\
& T_1=273 \mathrm{~K} \\
& P_2=760 \mathrm{~mm} \text { (given), } V_2=11.2 \text { it (given) } \\
& T_2=27+273=300 \mathrm{~K} \\
& \therefore \frac{760 \times 224}{32 \mathrm{~g} \times 273}=\frac{760 \times 11.2}{m_2 \times 300} \\
& m_2=\frac{32 \times 273}{300 \times 2}=14.56 \mathrm{~g} \\
& =0.01456 \mathrm{~kg} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Sinusoidal carrier voltage of frequency $1.5 \mathrm{MHz}$ and amplitude $50 \mathrm{~V}$ is amplitude modulated by sinusoidal voltage of frequency $10 \mathrm{kHz}$ producing $50 \%$ modulation. The lower and upper side-band frequencies in $\mathrm{kHz}$ are
questions.solution:
Here $f_{c}=1.5 \mathrm{MHz}=1500 \mathrm{kHz}, f_{m}=10 \mathrm{kHz}$
$\therefore$ Lower side-band frequency
$=f_{c}-f_{m}=1500 \mathrm{kHz}-10 \mathrm{kHz}=1490 \mathrm{kHz}$
Upper side-band frequency
$=f_{c}+f_{m}=1500 \mathrm{kHz}+10 \mathrm{kHz}=1510 \mathrm{kHz}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A frictionless piston-cylinder based enclosure contains some amount of gas at a pressure of $400 \mathrm{kPa}$. Then heat is transferred fo the gas at constant pressure in a quasi-static process. The piston moves up slowly through a height of $10 \mathrm{cm} .$ If the piston has a cross-sectional area of $0.3 \mathrm{m}^{2},$ the work done by the gas in this process is
questions.solution:
$W_{\text {constant pressure }}=p \times \Delta V$ $=400 \times 10^{3} \times 0.3 \times 10 \times 10^{-2}$
$=400 \times 10 \times 3$
$=12000=12 \mathrm{kJ}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The pressure and density of a given mass of a diatomic gas $\left(\gamma=\frac{7}{5}\right)$ change adiabatically from $(P, d)$ to $\left(P^{\prime}, d^{\prime}\right)$. If $\frac{d^{\prime}}{d}=32$, then $\frac{P^{\prime}}{P}$ is ( $\gamma=$ ratio of specific heats $)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & p V^\gamma=\frac{p}{\rho^\gamma}=\text { constant } \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{p^{\prime}}{p}=\left(\frac{\rho^{\prime}}{\rho}\right)=(32)^{7 / 5} \\ & =32 \times 4=128 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A system goes from A to B via two processes I and II shown in the figure. If ΔU1 and ΔU2 are the changes in internal energies in the processes I and Il respectively
questions.solution:
The process in which the initial and final state is the same is known as a cyclic process. It is a sequence of processes that leave the system in the same state in which it started.

Change in internal energy is independent on path, it depends on only initial and final state. So changes in internal energies for both process will be same.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following thermodynamical variables
(i) Pressure
(ii) Internal Energy
(iii) Volume
(iv) Temperature Out of these, the intensive variable(s) is (are)
questions.solution: An intensive property is that which does not depend on system size or amount of material in the system. So, pressure and temperature does not depend on system size, hence these are intensive properties.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: 3 moles of an ideal monoatomic gas performs $A B C D A$ cyclic process as shown in figure below. The gas temperatures are $T_A=400 \mathrm{~K}$, $T_B=800 \mathrm{~K}, T_C=2400 \mathrm{~K}$ and $T_D=1200 \mathrm{~K}$. The work done by the gas is (approximately) $(R=8.314 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{mol} \mathrm{K})$
questions.solution: Processes $A$ to $B$ and $C$ to $D$ are parts of straight line graphs of form $y=m x$. Also, $p=\frac{\mu R}{V} T(\mu=3)$ $p \propto T$ So, volume remains constant for the graphs $A B$ and $C D$. So, no work is done during processes for $A$ to $B$ and $C$ to $D$. $W_{A B}=W_{C D}=0$ and $\quad W_{B C}=p_2\left(V_C-V_B\right)$ $=\mu R\left(T_C-T_B\right)$ $=3 R(2400-800)$ $=3 R \times 1600$ $=4800 R$ $W_{D A}=p_1\left(V_A-V_D\right)$ $=\mu R\left(T_A-T_D\right)$ $=3 R(400-1200)$ $=-2400 R$ Work done in complete cycle $W=W_{A B}+W_{B C}+W_{C D}+W_{D A}$ $=0+4800 R+0+(-2400) R$ $=2400 R$ $=19.944 \mathrm{~J}=20 \mathrm{~kJ}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The pressure $p$, volume $V$ and temperature $T$ for a certain gas are related by $p=\frac{A T-B T^{2}}{V}$ where $A$ and $B$ are constants. The work done by the gas when the temperature changes from $T_{1}$ to $T_{2}$ while the pressure remains constant, is given by
questions.solution:
Given $P=\frac{A T-B T^{2}}{V}$
$\begin{array}{lc}\Rightarrow & P V=A T-B T^{2} \\ \Rightarrow & P \Delta V=A \Delta T-B T \Delta T\end{array}$
On integrating, we get Work $=\int P d V=A \int_{T_{1}}^{T_{2}} d T-B \int_{T_{1}}^{T_{2}} T d T$
$=A\left(T_{2}-T_{1}\right)-\frac{B}{2}\left[\left(T_{2}\right)^{2}-\left(T_{1}\right)^{2}\right]$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: 'A horizontal uniform glass tube of $100 \mathrm{~cm}$, length sealed at both ends contains $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ mercury column in the middle. The temperature and pressure of air on either side of mercury column are respectively $31^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $76 \mathrm{~cm}$ of mercury. If the air column at one end is kept at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and the other end at $273^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, then the pressure of air which is at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is : (in $\mathrm{cm}$ of $\mathrm{Hg}$ )
questions.solution:
On keeping the temperature of the ends of tube at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $273^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$

Applying ideal gas equation $\frac{p_1 V_1}{T_1}=\frac{p_2 V_2}{T_2}=\frac{p_3 V_3}{T_3}$ $\frac{76 \times 45}{(273+31)}=\frac{p_2 \times l}{(273+0)}=\frac{p_3(90-l)}{273+273}$ $\frac{76 \times 45}{304}=\frac{p_2 \times l}{273}=\frac{p_3(90-l)}{546}$ $\begin{array}{lll}&\text { I } &&\text { II } && \text { III }\end{array}$ From Part II and III, $\frac{p_2 \times l}{273}=\frac{p_3(90-l)}{546}$ (Mercury column is at rest, so pressure difference, $p_2-p_3=0 \Rightarrow p_2=p_3$ $\therefore \quad \frac{p_2 \times l}{273}=\frac{p_2(90-l)}{546}$ $2 l=90-l$ $\Rightarrow \quad l=30 \mathrm{~cm}$ From I and II, $\frac{76 \times 45}{304}=\frac{p_2 \times 30}{273}$ $\Rightarrow \quad p_2=\frac{76 \times 45 \times 273}{30 \times 304}$ $p_2=102.4$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: An ideal gas has molar heat capacity $C_V$ at constant volume. The gas undergoes a process where in the temperature changes as $T=T_0\left(1+\alpha V^2\right)$, where, $T$ and $V$ are temperature and volume respectively, $T_0$ and $\alpha$ are positive constants. The molar heat capacity $C$ of the gas is given as $C=C_V+R f(V)$, where, $f(V)$ is a function of volume. The expression for $f(V)$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $T=T_0\left(1+\alpha V^2\right)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d T}{d V}=\mathrm{T}_0 \cdot 2 \alpha V \\
& \Rightarrow \quad d V=\frac{d \mathrm{~T}}{\mathrm{~T}_0 \cdot 2 \alpha V}
\end{aligned}
$$
From first law of thermodynamics,
$$
\begin{array}{cc} 
& d Q=d U+d W \\
\Rightarrow & n C \cdot d T=n C_V \cdot d T+p d V \\
\Rightarrow & n C \cdot d T+n C_V \cdot d T+p\left(\frac{d T}{T_0 \cdot 2 \alpha V}\right) \\
\Rightarrow & C=C_V+\left(\frac{p}{n}\right)\left(\frac{1}{T_0 2 \alpha V}\right)
\end{array}
$$
Now as gas is ideal,
or
$$
\begin{aligned}
& p V=n R T \\
& \frac{p}{n}=\frac{R T}{V}=\frac{R\left[T_0\left(1+\alpha V^2\right)\right]}{V}
\end{aligned}
$$
substituting in Eq. (ii), we get,
$$
C=C_V+R \cdot\left(\frac{1+\alpha V^2}{\partial \alpha V^2}\right)
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A cylindrical adiabatic container of total volume 2V0 is divided into two equal parts by a conducting piston which is free to move as shown in the figure. Each part contains identical gas at pressure P0. Initially the temperatures of the left and right parts are 4T0 and T0 respectively. An external force is applied on the piston of area A to keep the piston at rest. The value of external force required when thermal equilibrium is reached is
questions.solution:
If final temp is T

P0V04RT0Cv4T0-T=P0V0RT0CvT-T0

4T0-T4=T-T0

4T0-T=4T-4T0

8T0=5T

T=8T05

Final pressure

In left Pf=TfT0Pi=85T04T0P0=25P0

In right Pf=85T0T0P0=85P0

Force=8P05-2P05A=6P0A5
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: P - V diagram of an ideal gas is shown. The gas undergoes from initial state A to final state B such that initial and final volumes are same. Select the correct alternative for given process AB .
options: Work done by gas is positive
Work done by gas is negative
questions.solution: Volume does not remain constant throughout the process AB. As T=PVnR, temperature decreases initially as both P and V decreases. By area under curve, net work done is negative.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
